mnemonics: False Sharing: F.A.S.T. (Frequently Accessed Same Tags) - Multiple cores frequently accessing the same cache line tags, causing invalidations
keyPoints: Cache coherence ensures all processors see a consistent view of shared memory
False sharing occurs when unrelated data resides in the same cache line, causing unnecessary invalidations
OpenMP provides padding and alignment directives to avoid false sharing
commonMistakes: Assuming private variables won't cause cache coherence issues
Not aligning data to cache line boundaries when needed
Using unnecessary critical sections instead of proper cache-conscious design
